query: 2026 Venezuela earthquakes June 24 confirmed death toll latest figures, trajectory of confirmed deaths reported by authorities and Wikipedia, number of missing persons and rescue operation status
answer:
I've completed the research on the June 24, 2026 Venezuela earthquakes. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Event:** Two earthquakes (Mw 7.2 foreshock + Mw 7.5 mainshock, 39 seconds apart) struck near Veroes Municipality, Yaracuy, on 24 June 2026. La Guaira state was devastated (~80% of buildings collapsed).

**Confirmed death toll trajectory** (a steep, continuous climb reported mainly by National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez and the health ministry):
- June 24: ~235 → June 29: 1,719 → ~July 1: 2,595 → July 6–7: **3,535** → ~July 8–9: ~3,900 → July 11: **more than 4,300** (Bloomberg) → **more than 4,400** (current Wikipedia lead)

**Missing:** "Tens of thousands"; ABC News reported nearly **50,000 unaccounted for** in early July. The wide gap between missing and confirmed dead signals the toll may keep rising as bodies are recovered.

**USGS PAGER:** projected the toll could exceed 10,000 (modeled range 10,000–100,000) — an estimate, not a confirmed count.

**Rescue status:** The 72-hour window passed ~June 29; notable late rescues occurred (86 hours on June 28, and 8 days on July 2). Around **July 8–9, operations transitioned from rescue to recovery**, with international teams departing and crews shifting to recovering remains.

**Displacement:** ~17,854–18,000 unhoused; ~12,800 in ~80 shelters; ~60,000 buildings damaged/destroyed; 27–28 countries providing aid under UN coordination.

**Key reconciliation note:** The July 7 figure of 3,535 has been superseded — the most recent confirmed figures (as of 2026-07-13) are **"more than 4,300" (Bloomberg, July 11)** and **"more than 4,400" (current Wikipedia lead)**. Full structured findings with the complete sourced timeline and data points have been submitted.

key_facts: Two earthquakes struck Venezuela on 24 June 2026: a Mw 7.2 foreshock at 18:04 VET followed 39 seconds later by a Mw 7.5 mainshock, epicentered in Veroes Municipality, Yaracuy.
La Guaira state was worst hit, with roughly 80% of buildings collapsed; ~60,000 buildings were damaged or destroyed nationwide.

Confirmed death toll rose steeply: ~235 (June 24) -> 1,719 (June 29) -> 2,595 (~July 1) -> 3,535 (July 6-7) -> ~3,900 (July 8-9) -> more than 4,300 (July 11) -> more than 4,400 (current Wikipedia lead).
Injured figure reached ~16,700-16,740 by early-to-mid July.
Tens of thousands reported missing; ABC News reported nearly 50,000 people unaccounted for in late June/early July 2026.
USGS PAGER projected the death toll could exceed 10,000, with modeled ranges of 10,000 to 100,000 (an estimate, not confirmed).
Around 17,854-18,000 people left unhoused; at least ~12,800 in about 80 shelters as of July 7.
27-28 countries provided assistance; over 2,000 rescue workers from 27 countries deployed under UN coordination.
Rescue operations transitioned to recovery around July 8-9, 2026, with international teams departing and crews shifting to recovering bodies.
Notable late rescues: a woman freed after 86 hours (June 28) and a man rescued 8 days after the quakes (July 2).
The mainshock was the strongest earthquake in Venezuela since the 1900 San Narciso earthquake.
Confirmed death toll is reported primarily by National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez and the health ministry.
